findings.title: 4 employee credentials exposed in breaches
findings.description: payroll@paytrust-partner.com, admin@..., ceo@... found in LinkedIn (2021), MyFitnessPal (2018), Collection#1.

findings.title: Redis port 6379 exposed to the public internet
findings.description: Unauthenticated Redis instance on 203.0.113.22:6379. Contains session tokens.

findings.title: TLS 1.0 enabled on customer-facing API
findings.description: api.paytrust-partner.com negotiates TLS 1.0. Deprecated and known-vulnerable.

findings.title: Domain flagged by 3/90 vendors as suspicious
findings.description: Two URL scanners report phishing look-alike domain activity.

findings.title: SPF record missing; DMARC policy: none
findings.description: Email spoofing protection absent.

findings.title: 18 subdomains discovered via CT logs
findings.description: Including `old-admin.paytrust-partner.com` (HTTP 200, no auth).

findings.title: CVE-2023-46604 (Apache ActiveMQ RCE) signature detected
findings.description: ProjectDiscovery nuclei matched http-cve-2023-46604 on mq.paytrust-partner.com.

findings.title: Privacy policy lacks DPDP §5 notice elements
findings.description: No mention of DPO, grievance channel or purposes in vendor's privacy policy.

findings.title: No published grievance officer (§8(8))
findings.description: No grievance-officer contact at /privacy or in app footer.

findings.title: 2 employee credentials in Collection#1
findings.description: dev@shopquick-vendor.com and ops@... found in Collection#1 breach.

findings.title: Elasticsearch 9200 exposed with no authentication
findings.description: Publicly accessible Elasticsearch index `orders` with 2.1M documents.

findings.title: 47 subdomains in CT logs
findings.description: Includes staging.* and test.* still serving production data.

findings.title: CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) detected on job-portal.shopquick-vendor.com
findings.description: Nuclei template cve-2021-44228 matched.

findings.title: Cross-border transfer to unlisted jurisdiction
findings.description: Data flows to ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) without §16 notification.

findings.title: 6 doctor accounts in breach corpora
findings.description: Six provider accounts found across 2 breaches.

findings.title: Self-signed certificate on api.healthbuddy-partner.com
findings.description: Cert not chained to a public CA; MITM possible.

findings.title: Exposed .git directory
findings.description: Nuclei matched git-config disclosure on main site.

findings.title: Children's data accepted without parental verification (§9)
findings.description: Paediatrics module collects minors' data with a checkbox only.

findings.title: No DPO appointed (§10)
findings.description: Vendor does not list DPO; by patient volume likely classified as SDF.

databridge-cloud.com.label: Cloud storage vendor with cross-border data flows

findings.title: MongoDB 27017 exposed, no authentication
findings.description: Unauthenticated MongoDB instance with 840K customer records cached in RAM.

findings.title: AWS S3 bucket publicly readable
findings.description: nuclei matched `aws-s3-bucket-findings` on backups.databridge-cloud.com.

findings.title: Primary storage region is us-east-1 (unlisted jurisdiction)
findings.description: Personal data of Indian users stored in the United States without §16 notification.

findings.title: Retention policy says 'indefinite' (§8(7))
findings.description: Terms retain user data 'as long as the account exists' with no post-closure purge.

findings.title: 3 engineering accounts in Collection#1 + LinkedIn 2021
findings.description: sre@databridge-cloud.com, devops@..., admin@... exposed.

findings.title: No data-processor agreement (DPA) referenced in contract
findings.description: Master agreement does not reference §8(4) purpose-limitation or sub-processor controls.

cleanpay-gateway.com.label: Payments vendor with mature security posture (safe baseline)

findings.title: 4 subdomains visible in CT logs (all production)
findings.description: Only production hostnames in Certificate Transparency logs; no stale staging or internal hosts leaked.

findings.title: DMARC policy is 'quarantine' not 'reject'
findings.description: SPF and DKIM are valid; DMARC enforcement could be stronger (p=reject recommended for payments vendors).

findings.title: Minor §6 data-minimisation observation
findings.description: Checkout form collects full date-of-birth when only year-of-birth is required for fraud scoring.

google-cloud-vendor.com.label: Enterprise cloud vendor — strong security posture (SAFE)

findings.title: 12 subdomains in CT logs (all production)
findings.description: Only production hostnames visible. Wildcard cert in use.

findings.title: DMARC policy is 'reject' — excellent email security
findings.description: SPF, DKIM, DMARC all properly configured with p=reject.

findings.title: 8 subdomains in CT logs (well-managed)
findings.description: Clean CT log footprint. No stale or internal hosts exposed.

findings.title: DMARC policy is 'quarantine' — good email hygiene
findings.description: SPF and DKIM valid. DMARC could be upgraded to reject.

findings.title: Minor §6 data-minimisation observation
findings.description: Registration collects job title when only company name is needed.

findings.title: 3 billion accounts compromised in 2013-2014 breaches
findings.description: Largest data breach in history. Employee and user credentials widely available on dark web.

findings.title: Legacy SMTP server with SSLv3 still active
findings.description: mail-legacy.yahoo-legacy-services.com negotiates SSLv3. POODLE-vulnerable.

findings.title: Domain flagged by 7/90 security vendors
findings.description: Multiple URL scanners flag phishing and malware distribution activity.

findings.title: TLS 1.0 and weak RC4 cipher still negotiated
findings.description: API endpoints negotiate TLS 1.0 with RC4-SHA. Known-broken encryption.

findings.title: No reasonable security safeguards (§8(5))
findings.description: Historical breach + legacy encryption + no MFA = systemic §8(5) failure.

findings.title: Breach notification timeline violated (§8(6))
findings.description: 2013 breach was not disclosed until 2016 — 3 years past any reasonable notification window.

findings.title: Cross-border transfer without adequate safeguards (§16)
findings.description: Data processed across 30+ international jurisdictions without DPIA.

findings.title: 147 million records exposed in 2017 breach
findings.description: SSNs, birth dates, addresses, and driver's license numbers exposed via Apache Struts vulnerability.

findings.title: CVE-2017-5638 (Apache Struts RCE) — original breach vector
findings.description: The exact vulnerability that caused the 2017 breach. Nuclei template matched on legacy endpoint.

findings.title: PostgreSQL 5432 exposed with default credentials
findings.description: Unauthenticated PostgreSQL instance with 2.3M credit records.

findings.title: No reasonable security safeguards (§8(5))
findings.description: Unpatched critical CVE + exposed database + massive breach = complete §8(5) failure.

findings.title: Sensitive personal data processed without adequate consent (§8(1))
findings.description: Credit scores and financial data processed without DPDP-compliant purpose limitation.

findings.title: No DPO appointed despite processing sensitive data (§10)
findings.description: Vendor handles sensitive personal data at scale but has no appointed Data Protection Officer.

logix-crm-india.com.label: Worst-case CRM vendor — maximum DPDP exposure (critical)

findings.title: MongoDB 27017 exposed with NO authentication
findings.description: Unauthenticated MongoDB instance on 203.0.113.77:27017. Full read/write. Contains 1.4M customer PII records including PAN + Aadhaar last-4.

findings.title: Admin credentials leaked across 3 breaches
findings.description: admin@logix-crm-india.com, root@..., support@... found in Collection#1, LinkedIn 2021, and Dropbox 2012. Same password hash reused across all three.

findings.title: Domain flagged malicious by 4/90 security vendors
findings.description: Four URL reputation engines mark logix-crm-india.com as phishing/malware distribution. Investigation recommended.

findings.title: Weak TLS configuration (TLS 1.0 + 3DES)
findings.description: api.logix-crm-india.com still negotiates TLS 1.0 with TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA. Known-vulnerable to SWEET32 / BEAST.

findings.title: 32 subdomains in CT logs — many stale / internal
findings.description: Includes admin-old.*, test-prod.*, internal-crm.*, dev-apis.* — all serving production data with HTTP 200.

findings.title: Missing DMARC record entirely
findings.description: No DMARC policy published. Domain is trivially spoofable for phishing that pivots into the CRM.

findings.title: No reasonable security safeguards (§8(5))
findings.description: Open MongoDB + leaked admin creds + weak TLS jointly constitute failure of §8(5) obligation to deploy reasonable security safeguards.

findings.title: Data-breach not reported to DPB within prescribed window (§8(6))
findings.description: Open-database exposure has persisted in Shodan for 47 days with no disclosure to the Data Protection Board or to affected Data Principals.

findings.title: Cross-border transfer to unlisted jurisdiction (§16)
findings.description: Primary replica hosted in us-east-1 and backups in eu-west-1. Neither jurisdiction is listed under the government's §16 notification. No DPIA filed.

apple-enterprise-vendor.com.label: Enterprise hardware & services vendor — strong security posture (SAFE)

findings.title: 6 subdomains in CT logs (all production, well-managed)
findings.description: Minimal CT log footprint. All entries are active production hosts with valid certificates.

findings.title: DMARC policy is 'reject' with RUA reporting — excellent
findings.description: SPF, DKIM, DMARC all configured with p=reject and aggregate reporting enabled.

tcs-outsource-vendor.com.label: IT outsourcing vendor — mixed security posture (mid-level)

findings.title: 5 employee accounts in breach corpora
findings.description: dev@tcs-outsource-vendor.com and 4 others found in LinkedIn 2021 and Collection#1.

findings.title: TLS 1.1 still negotiated on staging endpoint
findings.description: staging-api.tcs-outsource-vendor.com allows TLS 1.1 connections. Deprecated by RFC 8996.

findings.title: 22 subdomains in CT logs — 4 stale internal hosts
findings.description: dev-portal.*, internal-wiki.*, test-jenkins.*, old-monitoring.* still resolve and return HTTP 200.

findings.title: Cross-border transfer to multiple jurisdictions (§16)
findings.description: Service delivery from offices in India, US, UK, Singapore without unified DPIA.

findings.title: Sub-processor chain not disclosed (§8(4))
findings.description: Primary vendor sub-contracts to 3 undisclosed sub-processors without data principal notification.
